Misclustering/mapping conflict: B09ST_DT is described in raw data as 'DATE LAST STOPPED BCP'S' but is listed here as an exam date variable. The same variable (B09ST_DT and F09ST_DT) appears in BIRTH_CONTROL_STOP_DATE. It is likely that B09ST_DT and F09ST_DT belong to BIRTH_CONTROL_STOP_DATE and should not be in this concept. Recommend verifying whether this concept actually covers only G09CEXDAT (and possibly F09CEXDT) as exam dates.

This concept shares variable names (A13SCORE, C13SCORE) with COOK_MEDLEY_TOTAL_SCORE – both map to the same underlying variables and represent a misclustering. This concept should be suppressed in favor of COOK_MEDLEY_TOTAL_SCORE, which carries the more accurate label. The concept_id name ('GRAB_SCORE') appears to be a data entry error. The Exam A source description specifies 'first 50 questions'; the Exam C description says only 'Cook Medley Score' without this qualifier, suggesting possible scope change between exams or a labeling inconsistency.

Label and description corrected to reflect actual content. Additionally, varname B09HTTUS appears to be shared with the concept HEART_MED_TOTAL_MONTHS_USE, making this a likely duplicate. Suppressed (publish:false) pending resolution of the duplicate and mislabeling issues.

Variable F64SELF is assigned to both this concept and CHRONIC_BURDEN_SELF_HEALTH — confirmed duplicate assignment error. This concept groups two structurally distinct variables (F64SELF: serious health problems with yourself, Q1; F64HLTH: serious health problems with someone close, Q2) under one concept_id, which is a data structure artifact from Exam F where these items were combined before being split in later exams (G/H/I) into partner, parents, children, and others sub-categories. Recommend suppressing from publication and resolving the duplicate variable assignment and concept structure before publishing.

'derived_score' would imply a multi-item scale score; 'derived_value' is more appropriate for a binary indicator. Not suitable for publication as a standalone end-user variable.

YALTA_QC_PARTICIPANT_YN_EARLY shares the exact same varnames (F51YQC, G51YQC) for exams F and G, and both concepts cover those same exams. YALTA_QC_PARTICIPANT_YN additionally covers exams H and I. This is a serious misclustering issue: the F and G observations appear duplicated across two concept_ids. One concept should cover all four exams (F,G,H,I) or the early-wave split should be justified by different value labels or question wording. Until this is resolved, publishing both is problematic. Recommend consolidating into a single concept or clarifying the intended split.

Shares the exact same varnames (F51YQC, G51YQC) as YALTA_QC_PARTICIPANT_YN for exams F and G. These two concepts appear to duplicate the same underlying variables. YALTA_QC_PARTICIPANT_YN already covers exams F, G, H, and I. This early-wave concept is redundant and should not be published until the duplication is resolved. Recommend consolidating into YALTA_QC_PARTICIPANT_YN.

Two varnames listed (G51Y03ML, G51YCTB1) for a single exam (G). G51YCTB1 shares a base name with H51YCTB1 and I51YCTB1, which are the varnames for YALTA_URINE_3ML_ALIQUOTS, suggesting G51YCTB1 may belong in that concept rather than here. Only value label is '0 = Empty'. Recommend resolving the varname overlap and potential misclustering before publishing.
